{
  "@context": "https://schema.org",
  "@type": "JobPosting",
  "title": "Fleet Officer",
  "description": "Cen Global Services Limited (&ldquo;Cen Global&rdquo;) is proudly a Nigerian registered company with broad specialization in Shipping &amp; Marine, Recruitment &amp; Manpower Supply, Procurement &amp; Engineering Services, and Consultancy Services. Cen Global was incorporated in March, 2010.

Our business sustainable approach is to understand the needs of our clients and subsequently create a uniquely structured service process tailored towards meeting or exceeding expectations. Our Corporate Head office is located in Port Harcourt, Rivers State, Nigeria, but we have developed a robust network around the country to effectively deliver products and services across Nigeria at very short notice.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Fleet Officer

Job Location:&nbsp;Onitsha, Anambra
Employment Type: Full-time

Job Description


	We are seeking a dependable and detail-oriented Fleet Officer to manage day-to-day transportation and logistics operations inOnitsha,Anambra State.
	The ideal candidate is proactive, organized, and committed to ensuring the smooth and cost-effective movement of people, goods, and services within and outside the state.


Key Responsibilities


	Coordinate and monitor all fleet and logistics activities to ensure efficiency and cost-effectiveness.
	Maintain accurate documentation of all company vehicles, drivers, and logistics activities.
	Schedule and dispatch vehicles and drivers to ensure timely deliveries and pickups.
	Track and monitor vehicle movement using GPS or fleet management tools.
	Manage fuel usage, trip records, and driver performance reports.
	Ensure all vehicles comply with transportation regulations, insurance, and company safety policies.
	Collaborate with vendors, clients, and internal departments to support seamless logistics operations.
	Provide periodic reports on fleet performance, maintenance, and operational costs.


Requirements


	A good Bachelors Degree in any field
	2 - 4 Years of proven experience as a fleet officer
	Strong understanding of route planning, vehicle tracking systems, and transportation regulations.
	Working knowledge of relevant transport and compliance standards in Nigeria.
	Excellent organizational, communication, and problem-solving skills.
	Must reside in onitsha.


Method of Application&nbsp;
Interested and qualified candidates should send their&nbsp;CVS&nbsp;and&nbsp;Cover Letters&nbsp;to:&nbsp;careers@cenglobalservices.com&nbsp;using the Job Position as the subject of the mail.",

Fleet Officer Job at Cen Global Services Limited




        
    

        
        Cen Global Services Limited (“Cen Global”) is proudly a Nigerian registered company with broad specialization in Shipping & Marine, Recruitment & Manpower Supply, Procurement & Engineering Services, and Consultancy Services. Cen Global was incorporated in March, 2010.

Our business sustainable approach is to understand the needs of our clients and subsequently create a uniquely structured service process tailored towards meeting or exceeding expectations. Our Corporate Head office is located in Port Harcourt, Rivers State, Nigeria, but we have developed a robust network around the country to effectively deliver products and services across Nigeria at very short notice.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:
     

        



     (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({});
    
    



Job Position: Fleet Officer

Job Location: Onitsha, Anambra
Employment Type: Full-time

Job Description


	We are seeking a dependable and detail-oriented Fleet Officer to manage day-to-day transportation and logistics operations inOnitsha,Anambra State.
	The ideal candidate is proactive, organized, and committed to ensuring the smooth and cost-effective movement of people, goods, and services within and outside the state.


Key Responsibilities


	Coordinate and monitor all fleet and logistics activities to ensure efficiency and cost-effectiveness.
	Maintain accurate documentation of all company vehicles, drivers, and logistics activities.
	Schedule and dispatch vehicles and drivers to ensure timely deliveries and pickups.
	Track and monitor vehicle movement using GPS or fleet management tools.
	Manage fuel usage, trip records, and driver performance reports.
	Ensure all vehicles comply with transportation regulations, insurance, and company safety policies.
	Collaborate with vendors, clients, and internal departments to support seamless logistics operations.
	Provide periodic reports on fleet performance, maintenance, and operational costs.


Requirements


	A good Bachelors Degree in any field
	2 - 4 Years of proven experience as a fleet officer
	Strong understanding of route planning, vehicle tracking systems, and transportation regulations.
	Working knowledge of relevant transport and compliance standards in Nigeria.
	Excellent organizational, communication, and problem-solving skills.
	Must reside in onitsha.


Method of Application 
Interested and qualified candidates should send their CVS and Cover Letters to: [email protected] using the Job Position as the subject of the mail.    

    

        Click here to Apply

{
  "@context": "https://schema.org",
  "@type": "JobPosting",
  "title": "Business Officer",
  "description": "Bosak Microfinance Bank Limited was incorporated in December 2009 with the business objective of providing financial service to micro clients who were excluded from the mainstream financial system. It commenced operations in June 2010 upon obtaining a Unit Microfinance Banking License from the Central Bank of Nigeria. It later obtained a State Microfinance Banking License in 2016.

Bosak Microfinance Bank Limited&rsquo;s financial inclusion effort focuses on promoting savings culture, access to credit facilities for income-generation purposes and reducing vulnerability to risk through micro insurance.

The Bank&rsquo;s major strategic focus employs the group lending methodology in its operations with a leaning towards women. It believes in the commitment of women folk to work hard and help their families conquer poverty. As such, more than 95% of the Bank&rsquo;s lending activities focuses on women. The success of the bank&rsquo;s group lending activities largely depends on group solidarity and cohesion.

The success recorded by the bank in its lending program has earned it strategic partnership with the Bank of Industry (BOI) and the Development Bank of Nigeria (DBN) among others. The combination of cutting-edge technology, a strong and committed Board of Directors and motivated employees has helped the Bank to create a brand widely accepted by its teeming clients in Lagos. It is now expanding its frontiers in a scaling program aimed at delivering improved value to its stakeholders.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Business Officer

Job Location:&nbsp;Ojota, Lagos
Employment Type: Full-time

Job Description


	Approve loan applications within specified approval limits and refer applications outside approved limits to management for further approval.
	Meet with loan applicants to obtain required information and respond to inquiries regarding the loan application process.
	Analyze applicants&rsquo; financial status, credit history, and collateral/property valuations to determine loan feasibility.
	Explain available loan products, credit options, and applicable terms and conditions to customers.
	Obtain, verify, and compile loan applicants&rsquo; credit histories, corporate financial statements, and other relevant financial documentation.
	Review, update, and maintain accurate credit and loan files in line with internal policies and regulatory requirements.
	Ensure compliance with credit policies, risk management guidelines, and regulatory standards.
	Provide periodic reports on loan portfolio performance as required.
	Field picking up of loans.


Remuneration


	Salary: N100,000 &ndash; N140,000 Monthly.
	HMO
	Constant Training and Development
	Leave &amp; Passage Allowance
	13th Month.


Why Work with Bosak Microfinance Bank Limited?


	If you desire to work in an organization where employee motivation is key and passion is rewarded, then Bosak MFB might be the place for you.
	We are committed to building an open and rewarding work environment where all employees have equal opportunities for growth to realize their full potential.
	Our remuneration and employee welfare packages are among the best in the industry.


Method of Application&nbsp;
Interested and qualified candidates should send their CV to:&nbsp;recruitment@bosakmfb.com&nbsp;using the Job Position as the subject of the email.",

Business Officer Job at Bosak Microfinance Bank Limited




        
    

        
        Bosak Microfinance Bank Limited was incorporated in December 2009 with the business objective of providing financial service to micro clients who were excluded from the mainstream financial system. It commenced operations in June 2010 upon obtaining a Unit Microfinance Banking License from the Central Bank of Nigeria. It later obtained a State Microfinance Banking License in 2016.

Bosak Microfinance Bank Limited’s financial inclusion effort focuses on promoting savings culture, access to credit facilities for income-generation purposes and reducing vulnerability to risk through micro insurance.
     

        



     (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({});
    
    



The Bank’s major strategic focus employs the group lending methodology in its operations with a leaning towards women. It believes in the commitment of women folk to work hard and help their families conquer poverty. As such, more than 95% of the Bank’s lending activities focuses on women. The success of the bank’s group lending activities largely depends on group solidarity and cohesion.

The success recorded by the bank in its lending program has earned it strategic partnership with the Bank of Industry (BOI) and the Development Bank of Nigeria (DBN) among others. The combination of cutting-edge technology, a strong and committed Board of Directors and motivated employees has helped the Bank to create a brand widely accepted by its teeming clients in Lagos. It is now expanding its frontiers in a scaling program aimed at delivering improved value to its stakeholders.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Business Officer

Job Location: Ojota, Lagos
Employment Type: Full-time

Job Description


	Approve loan applications within specified approval limits and refer applications outside approved limits to management for further approval.
	Meet with loan applicants to obtain required information and respond to inquiries regarding the loan application process.
	Analyze applicants’ financial status, credit history, and collateral/property valuations to determine loan feasibility.
	Explain available loan products, credit options, and applicable terms and conditions to customers.
	Obtain, verify, and compile loan applicants’ credit histories, corporate financial statements, and other relevant financial documentation.
	Review, update, and maintain accurate credit and loan files in line with internal policies and regulatory requirements.
	Ensure compliance with credit policies, risk management guidelines, and regulatory standards.
	Provide periodic reports on loan portfolio performance as required.
	Field picking up of loans.


Remuneration


	Salary: N100,000 – N140,000 Monthly.
	HMO
	Constant Training and Development
	Leave & Passage Allowance
	13th Month.


Why Work with Bosak Microfinance Bank Limited?


	If you desire to work in an organization where employee motivation is key and passion is rewarded, then Bosak MFB might be the place for you.
	We are committed to building an open and rewarding work environment where all employees have equal opportunities for growth to realize their full potential.
	Our remuneration and employee welfare packages are among the best in the industry.


Method of Application 
Interested and qualified candidates should send their CV to: [email protected] using the Job Position as the subject of the email.    

    

        Click here to Apply

title: Job Openings at Wemy Industries Limited
content:
Wemy Industries is one of the foremost indigenous and oldest established manufacturers in Lagos, Nigeria. We specialize in producing baby diapers, feminine sanitary napkins/pads, panty liners, adult diapers, maternity pads, under pads and baby wipes under the umbrella brand names of ‘Dr. Browns’ and ‘Nightingale’

We are recruiting to fill the following positions below:

1.) Production Supervisor

Job Location: Ketu, Lagos

Job Responsibilities
     

        



     (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({});
    
    




	Supervise and coordinate activities of machine operators, packers, and line workers.
	Ensure daily/shift production targets are met with minimal downtime.
	Monitor line performance and quickly resolve operational issues.
	Enforce adherence to standard operating procedures (SOPs) across all production stages.
	Ensure diapers meet quality specifications. Conduct routine quality checks and collaborate with QA team on non-conformities.
	Implement corrective and preventive actions for defects or recurring issues.
	Oversee the operation of key machines (cutting, SAP application, elastic bands, sealing, packaging, etc.).
	Report mechanical faults and ensure timely maintenance.
	Monitor usage of raw materials (tissue, non-woven, SAP, elastic, tapes, packaging).
	Prevent wastage and ensure optimal utilization.
	Communicate material shortages and support inventory planning.
	Assign tasks, manage shift schedules, and supervise attendance.
	Enforce compliance with company policies and regulatory standards.
	Report accidents/incidents and follow proper investigation procedures.
	Maintain daily production logs, downtime reports, and material consumption records.
	Present shift performance reports to management.
	Support audits and documentation requirements.


Qualifications & Requirements


	HND/BSc in Engineering, Industrial Management, or related field.
	3 – 5 years of experience in FMCG manufacturing; diaper or hygiene product experience is an advantage.
	Strong understanding of high-speed production machines.
	Good leadership, communication, and problem-solving skills.
	Ability to work shifts, weekends, and under pressure.


Method of Application
Interested and qualified candidates should send their CV to: [email protected] using the Job Title as the subject of the email.

 

 

2.) Medical Sales Representative

Job Location: Abuja

Job Summary


	The Medical Sales Representative will be responsible for promoting and selling the company’s healthcare and hygiene products to healthcare professionals, including doctors, pharmacists, and hospitals.
	This role involves building strong relationships, increasing product awareness, expanding customer coverage, and achieving sales targets within assigned territory.


Key Responsibilities


	Promote and detail the company’s products to healthcare professionals such as doctors, pharmacists, and medical institutions.
	Build and maintain strong relationships with key stakeholders in hospitals, clinics, and pharmacies.
	Achieve assigned sales targets and expand product market share within the territory.
	Conduct regular field visits to healthcare facilities to drive product awareness and adoption.
	Organize and participate in medical presentations, seminars, and product launches.
	Monitor competitor activities, pricing, and market trends.
	Provide accurate product information, including benefits, usage, and potential side effects.
	Maintain detailed records of customer interactions, sales activities, and market intelligence.
	Ensure compliance with industry regulations and company ethical standards.
	Submit periodic sales reports and forecasts to management.


Requirements & Qualifications


	Minimum of B.Sc. in Pharmacy, Biochemistry, Microbiology, or any related life science field.
	3-5 years’ experience in hygiene products, medical or pharmaceutical sales.
	Strong knowledge of hygiene products and the healthcare industry.
	Excellent communication, presentation, negotiation, and persuasion skills.
	Ability to build and maintain professional relationships.
	Self-motivated and target driven.
	Proficiency in Microsoft Office tools.
	Valid driver’s license.


Method of Application 
Interested and qualified candidates should send their CV to: [email protected] using the Job Position as the subject of the email.

 

 

3.) Senior Cost Accountant

Job Location: Lagos

Job Summary


	The Senior Cost Accountant will be responsible for planning, analyzing, and controlling manufacturing costs to support profitability and operational efficiency.
	The role involves cost tracking, inventory valuation, budgeting, variance analysis, and providing cost insights to management for strategic decision-making.


Key Responsibilities


	Develop and maintain standard costing systems for raw materials, WIP, and finished goods.
	Monitor production costs and identify areas of wastage, inefficiencies, and cost reduction opportunities.
	Conduct periodic review of product costing and recommend pricing adjustments where necessary.
	Ensure accurate allocation of overheads across production lines and cost centers.
	Ensure proper valuation of raw materials, WIP, and finished goods in line with IFRS/company policy.
	Reconcile inventory balances between ERP/system records and physical stock.
	Support periodic stock counts (monthly/quarterly/yearly) and investigate variances.
	Track consumption of materials and monitor inventory turnover.
	Prepare manufacturing cost budgets and support annual financial planning.
	Work with production and procurement teams to forecast material and production costs.
	Monitor budget performance and report variances with explanations and corrective actions.
	Perform variance analysis on material, labor, and overhead costs (standard vs actual).
	Prepare monthly cost reports, production efficiency reports, and margin analysis.
	Provide management with insights on cost trends and profitability by product line.
	Support month-end and year-end closing processes relating to inventory and production cost.
	Ensure compliance with internal controls and accounting policies.
	Maintain accurate documentation for audit purposes.
	Support external and internal audits related to costing and inventory.
	Support implementation and improvement of ERP/accounting systems for cost tracking.
	Recommend process improvements in cost reporting, stock management, and production efficiency.
	Collaborate with production, procurement, warehouse, and finance teams to strengthen cost accountability.


Qualifications & Experience


	B.Sc / HND in Accounting, Finance, Business Administration, or related discipline.
	Minimum of 4 – 8 years of accounting experience, with at least 3 years in manufacturing cost accounting.
	ACA/ACCA/CIMA is strongly preferred.
	Strong understanding of inventory valuation, standard costing, and variance analysis.
	Experience with ERP systems (SAP, Oracle, Microsoft Dynamics, Sage, Odoo, etc.) is an advantage.


Method of Application 
Interested and qualified candidates should send their CV to: [email protected] using the Job Position as the subject of the email.    

    

        Click here to Apply

{
  "@context": "https://schema.org",
  "@type": "JobPosting",
  "title": "Customer Service Manager",
  "description": "AAVA Brands Limited, a new generation company of Chanrai Summit Group (Fareast Mercantile Co. Limited), has been proudly serving Nigeria since 1890 and connects world-class brands to the Nigerian consumer, every day. Now under the umbrella of DP World, AAVA Brands Limited is further strengthened as a flexible and agile service provider. Keeping pace with the evolving needs of African consumers, we strive harder to make the right products available at the right place, in the right time.

We have dedicated our resources to supply chain management through the import / manufacture and distribution of products covering branded Food, Beverages and Fast Moving Consumer Goods for the best multi-nationals in the world. A product portfolio spanning 3,500 SKUs is managed by capable, professional teams, robust infrastructure, and strong delivery systems to ensure availability to consumers every day. As a socially responsible group, we have partnered with various NGOs such as the Tulsi Chanrai Foundation to support their social welfare initiatives.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Customer Service Manager

Job Locations:&nbsp;Abuja, Port Harcourt - Rivers, Ibadan - Oyo and Lagos

Responsibilities


	Ensure customer service team is available as per set timings every day.
	Drive discipline of compliance to office hours, timely response on emails and phone calls.
	Drive high productivity among team members &ndash; measure no. of calls made, and emails responded to / day / person, maintain minimum possible, open issues on hand.
	Lead collaboration with stakeholders for timely issue resolution.
	Continuously follow up and remind stakeholders of key open complaints every month.
	Monthly / weekly communication on status of issues resolved and open issues.
	Circulate daily, weekly, monthly reports to give visibility on key metrics and seek actions pending from others.


Requirements&nbsp;


	Any graduate / HND holder with 3-5 years of relevant work experience&nbsp;in customer service as a supervisor.

Customer Service Manager Job at AAVA Brands Limited




        
    

        
        AAVA Brands Limited, a new generation company of Chanrai Summit Group (Fareast Mercantile Co. Limited), has been proudly serving Nigeria since 1890 and connects world-class brands to the Nigerian consumer, every day. Now under the umbrella of DP World, AAVA Brands Limited is further strengthened as a flexible and agile service provider. Keeping pace with the evolving needs of African consumers, we strive harder to make the right products available at the right place, in the right time.
     

        



     (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({});
    
    



We have dedicated our resources to supply chain management through the import / manufacture and distribution of products covering branded Food, Beverages and Fast Moving Consumer Goods for the best multi-nationals in the world. A product portfolio spanning 3,500 SKUs is managed by capable, professional teams, robust infrastructure, and strong delivery systems to ensure availability to consumers every day. As a socially responsible group, we have partnered with various NGOs such as the Tulsi Chanrai Foundation to support their social welfare initiatives.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Customer Service Manager

Job Locations: Abuja, Port Harcourt - Rivers, Ibadan - Oyo and Lagos

Responsibilities


	Ensure customer service team is available as per set timings every day.
	Drive discipline of compliance to office hours, timely response on emails and phone calls.
	Drive high productivity among team members – measure no. of calls made, and emails responded to / day / person, maintain minimum possible, open issues on hand.
	Lead collaboration with stakeholders for timely issue resolution.
	Continuously follow up and remind stakeholders of key open complaints every month.
	Monthly / weekly communication on status of issues resolved and open issues.
	Circulate daily, weekly, monthly reports to give visibility on key metrics and seek actions pending from others.


Requirements 


	Any graduate / HND holder with 3-5 years of relevant work experience in customer service as a supervisor.
    

    

        Click here to Apply
